title: AI in Decision Making

modules.title: Automated Decision Systems
modules.sub: How AI systems reach conclusions that shape real outcomes
modules.description: Learners examine how automated decision systems are designed, trained, and deployed across industries such as finance, healthcare, and hiring. They analyze the logic behind algorithmic outputs and identify the conditions under which those outputs can be trusted or questioned. By the end of this module, learners can distinguish between rule-based and machine learning decision models and articulate their practical implications.

modules.title: Human-AI Collaboration
modules.sub: Defining the right balance between human and machine judgment
modules.description: Learners explore frameworks for structuring effective collaboration between human professionals and AI systems, deciding which tasks to delegate and which to retain. They work through scenarios that reveal how cognitive biases and automation complacency can erode decision quality when humans over-rely on AI recommendations. Learners leave with a practical model for calibrating human involvement based on risk, context, and system reliability.

modules.title: Decision Quality and Accuracy
modules.sub: Measuring whether AI-driven decisions are actually reliable
modules.description: Learners apply evaluation methods to assess the accuracy, consistency, and fairness of AI-generated decisions across different population groups and use cases. They interpret performance metrics such as precision, recall, and error rates in business and policy contexts, connecting technical outputs to real-world consequences. This module equips learners to critically audit AI decision quality rather than accept system outputs at face value.

modules.title: Accountability in AI Decisions
modules.sub: Assigning responsibility when AI influences consequential outcomes
modules.description: Learners investigate governance structures, legal frameworks, and organizational policies that determine who is accountable when an AI-assisted decision causes harm. They examine case studies where accountability gaps led to significant consequences and design oversight mechanisms suited to their own professional contexts. Learners conclude by drafting a set of accountability principles they can apply to AI deployments within their organizations.

synopsis: Examine how AI systems make decisions that affect people's lives. Learn when to trust AI recommendations and how to maintain human oversight.
